title: Quiz: Data Encoding Overview
description: Test your understanding of quantum data encoding strategies

questions.question: Which encoding method achieves the best qubit efficiency for storing N continuous values?

options.text: All require the same number of qubits

questions.explanation: Amplitude encoding requires only log₂(N) qubits to store N values as amplitudes, achieving exponential compression compared to angle encoding which needs N qubits.

questions.question: What is the main drawback of amplitude encoding?

options.text: General state preparation requires O(N) gates

questions.explanation: While amplitude encoding uses few qubits, preparing an arbitrary amplitude-encoded state generally requires O(N) gates, potentially negating the space advantage.

questions.question: Data re-uploading refers to encoding the data multiple times with variational layers in between.
questions.explanation: True! Data re-uploading interleaves encoding layers with variational layers, allowing the circuit to build more complex representations of the input data.

questions.question: For variational quantum classifiers with continuous features, which encoding is most commonly used?
questions.explanation: Angle encoding with entangling layers is the standard choice for VQC because it's easy to implement, scales naturally with features, and provides good expressivity.

questions.question: Which factors should influence your choice of encoding? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Type of input data (binary, continuous, etc.)
The color of the quantum computer

questions.explanation: Encoding choice depends on data type, qubit availability, and depth constraints. The physical appearance of hardware is irrelevant to encoding choice.
